GlobalPolicy.module-level-focus: Fast deterministic checks for vvoc config layout, managed agent prompt scaffolding, managed agent registration, preset application, main-session system guidance injection, Guardian config rendering, memory CRUD, reviewer registration, and secrets redaction.
GlobalPolicy.wave-level-focus: Typecheck, lint, format-check, and test the merged CLI + plugin surface after each coordinated change.
GlobalPolicy.phase-level-focus: Buildable package output plus stable command behavior for installation, enhancer prompting, preset switching, primary-session system guidance, Guardian review, explicit memory workflows, and secrets redaction.
GlobalPolicy.redaction: Never log secrets, credentials, raw tokens, or high-risk payloads outside the existing Guardian truncation policy.

VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.NAME: PluginInstall
VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.scenario: User runs `vvoc init` to bootstrap the package into a fresh global or project config root.
VF-PLUGIN-INSTALL.expected-outcome: The pinned vvoc package specifier is present in the resolved OpenCode `plugin` array, the managed enhancer agent and managed subagents are registered in OpenCode config, managed prompts exist in the scope-correct agents directory, and the canonical `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json` file is fully seeded.

required-signals.trace-sequence: resolvePaths -> ensurePackageInstalled -> syncManagedAgentRegistrations -> installManagedAgentPrompts -> installVvocConfig -> status reports configured

VF-CONFIG-SYNC.NAME: ManagedConfigSync
VF-CONFIG-SYNC.scenario: User syncs managed vvoc config after changing package versions or moving to another device.
VF-CONFIG-SYNC.expected-outcome: Canonical vvoc.json and managed prompt files are rewritten canonically, the managed enhancer/subagent registration stays present in OpenCode config, legacy v1 docs migrate to v2 with seeded default presets, and current explicit values survive the vvoc.json rewrite where applicable.

required-signals.trace-sequence: inspect canonical config -> parse current values -> render canonical JSON/markdown -> syncManagedAgentRegistrations

VF-PRESET-SWITCH.NAME: PresetSwitch
VF-PRESET-SWITCH.scenario: User applies a named preset such as `vvoc preset openai` or `vvoc preset zai`.
VF-PRESET-SWITCH.expected-outcome: Only the targets declared in the selected preset change, unlisted targets remain untouched, and existing vvoc/OpenCode write paths are reused for canonical and OpenCode-managed targets including top-level OpenCode model defaults.

required-signals.trace-sequence: read canonical vvoc.json -> resolve preset -> write listed guardian/memory/OpenCode target overrides -> print per-target summary

VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.NAME: GuardianReview
VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.scenario: OpenCode asks for a permission decision while Guardian is active.
VF-GUARDIAN-REVIEW.expected-outcome: Low-risk actions may be auto-approved and higher-risk actions fall back to manual approval.

VF-MEMORY-ACCESS.NAME: MemoryAccess
VF-MEMORY-ACCESS.scenario: User stores and retrieves explicit memory across session, branch, project, and shared scopes.
VF-MEMORY-ACCESS.expected-outcome: Session, branch, and project entries stay project-local, shared entries are visible across projects, and stored memory is never injected into prompts automatically, while the agent still receives a short system reminder to use memory tools proactively.

required-signals.trace-sequence: system reminder appended -> loadMemoryRuntimeConfig -> putMemory/listMemories/searchMemories -> correct local/shared file layout

VF-MEMORY-REVIEW.NAME: MemoryReview
VF-MEMORY-REVIEW.scenario: User invokes `@memory-reviewer` to audit existing memory.
VF-MEMORY-REVIEW.expected-outcome: The reviewer can inspect memory with read-only tools and returns a report without changing entries.

required-signals.trace-sequence: plugin config registers memory-reviewer -> reviewer uses memory_list/get/search only

VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.NAME: SystemContextInjection
VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.scenario: User starts a primary chat session with vvoc installed.
VF-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.expected-outcome: The main-session user message carries reusable vvoc system guidance such as proactive explore usage, while known subagents do not inherit that guidance.

required-signals.trace-sequence: chat.message -> resolved primary agent -> append message.system guidance -> skip known subagents

VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.NAME: SecretsRedaction
VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.scenario: LLM request is sent with secrets in message history, tool inputs, or tool outputs.
VF-SECRETS-REDACTION.expected-outcome: Secrets are replaced with `__VVOC_SECRET_*` placeholders before LLM request, restored after LLM response, and restored before tool execution.

scenarios.scenario-1: Fresh install config renders with schema and a pinned vvoc package specifier in the plugin array.
scenarios.scenario-2: Existing JSONC comments survive plugin insertion.
scenarios.scenario-3: Guardian section rendering round-trips explicit override values.
scenarios.scenario-4: Global path resolution separates `opencode/` from the canonical `vvoc/vvoc.json` path while project scope still uses `./.vvoc/agents/` for prompts.
scenarios.scenario-5: Managed enhancer/subagent registrations point OpenCode at `./.vvoc/agents/*.md` or `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/agents/*.md` with scope-correct relative prompt references and correct `primary` versus `subagent` modes.
scenarios.scenario-6: Canonical vvoc.json is fully seeded, section writes preserve unrelated sections, managed prompt files are scaffolded in the scope-correct agents directory, model overrides round-trip through OpenCode config for both vvoc-managed agents and the built-in `general` and `explore` agents, and only guardian keeps an explicit `steps` limit.
scenarios.scenario-7: Provider baseURL patching preserves JSONC comments and writes `provider.stepfun.options.baseURL` conservatively.
scenarios.scenario-8: Schema v2 accepts the `presets` map, shared agent validation rejects malformed special-agent model syntax, and strict reads normalize legacy v1 documents until sync rewrites them to v2.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Sync must rewrite the canonical vvoc.json file instead of depending on separate guardian, memory, or secrets-redaction config files.

V-M-CLI-CONFIG.wave-follow-up: Smoke command output for `status` and `doctor` after build.
V-M-CLI-CONFIG.phase-follow-up: Extend tests to cover more path and scope permutations.

scenarios.scenario-1: CLI compiles and registers all planned top-level subcommands including `version`, `patch-provider`, and `preset`.
scenarios.scenario-2: `guardian config --print` emits valid guardian section JSON content.
scenarios.scenario-3: `vvoc agent list` reports top-level `default` and `small-model`, Guardian, memory-reviewer, the built-in `general` and `explore` agents, the managed `enhancer` primary agent, and the four vvoc-managed OpenCode subagents.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Command handlers should delegate file mutation to helper utilities instead of duplicating filesystem logic.

V-M-CLI-COMMANDS.wave-follow-up: Verify `vvoc install`, `vvoc sync`, and `vvoc doctor` against a temp config home.
V-M-CLI-COMMANDS.phase-follow-up: Add command-level smoke tests once temp-dir fixtures are introduced.

scenarios.scenario-1: Guardian plugin exports compile against current OpenCode plugin and SDK types, and plugin config registers guardian as a hidden subagent with an explicit two-step limit.
scenarios.scenario-2: Review failures fall back to manual approval instead of silently allowing.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Auto-allow only occurs when Guardian returns a risk score below the configured threshold.

V-M-PLUGIN-GUARDIAN.wave-follow-up: Add isolated behavioral tests around assessment parsing and decision thresholds.
V-M-PLUGIN-GUARDIAN.phase-follow-up: Consider extracting pure Guardian parsing helpers for direct unit coverage.

scenarios.scenario-1: Session, branch, and project entries stay project-local while shared entries are visible across projects.
scenarios.scenario-2: Search returns the best lexical match in the requested scope.
scenarios.scenario-3: Update and delete preserve store consistency and clean empty directories.
scenarios.scenario-4: Legacy project-local `shared` directories are ignored after the scope semantic change.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Memory stays explicit-only and no store operation injects entries into prompts automatically.

V-M-PLUGIN-MEMORY-STORE.wave-follow-up: Watch performance if the number of JSON entries grows significantly.
V-M-PLUGIN-MEMORY-STORE.phase-follow-up: Consider indexing only when the simple file scan becomes a measured problem.

scenarios.scenario-1: MemoryPlugin registers all six memory tools.
scenarios.scenario-2: Plugin config registers the `memory-reviewer` subagent without an explicit `steps` limit.
scenarios.scenario-3: Plugin appends a short system instruction encouraging proactive memory tool usage without injecting stored entries.
scenarios.scenario-4: Disabled memory config blocks tool use instead of pretending memory exists.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The reviewer agent must not have write/delete memory tools enabled.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The system reminder must not include stored memory entries or imply that memory is already loaded.

V-M-PLUGIN-MEMORY.wave-follow-up: Consider adding a smoke test around custom tool execution in a live OpenCode session.
V-M-PLUGIN-MEMORY.phase-follow-up: Add coverage for memory config warnings and disabled mode if the config surface grows.

scenarios.scenario-1: Primary agents such as `build` and `enhancer` receive the injected system guidance exactly once.
scenarios.scenario-2: Known subagents such as `explore`, `implementer`, and `memory-reviewer` do not receive the injected guidance.
scenarios.scenario-3: Custom config-defined subagents stay excluded after the plugin observes config mode metadata.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The plugin must mutate `message.system` through `chat.message`, not through the global system transform hook, so agent filtering remains possible.

V-M-PLUGIN-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.wave-follow-up: Consider making the injected system contexts configurable once multiple reusable guidance blocks exist.
V-M-PLUGIN-SYSTEM-CONTEXT-INJECTION.phase-follow-up: Revisit implementation if OpenCode exposes agent information on `experimental.chat.system.transform`.

scenarios.scenario-1: Plugin exports compile against current OpenCode plugin and SDK types.
scenarios.scenario-2: Placeholders are stable within a session (same secret maps to same placeholder).
scenarios.scenario-3: Builtin patterns detect email, uuid, ipv4, mac, openai_key, anthropic_key, github_token, aws_access_key, stripe_key, bearer_token, bearer_dot, syn_key, hex_token.
scenarios.scenario-4: Secrets-redaction config loads from the `secretsRedaction` section in $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/vvoc/vvoc.json.
scenarios.scenario-5: Disabled config makes plugin a no-op.
scenarios.scenario-6: A user text part containing a builtin secret such as an email is replaced with a `__VVOC_SECRET_*` placeholder before model message conversion.
scenarios.scenario-7: Exact placeholders are restored in assistant text completions and tool arguments before they reach the user or the tool runtime.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: HMAC hash is irreversible without the session secret.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-3: Max-mappings eviction removes oldest entries when limit is reached.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-4: Overlap handling must not skip the first match or prefer a later inner match over a wider earlier secret span.

V-M-PLUGIN-SECRETS-REDACTION.wave-follow-up: Add live session smoke coverage once a safe harness exists for observing provider-bound payloads.
V-M-PLUGIN-SECRETS-REDACTION.phase-follow-up: Consider opt-in safe debug logs if future redaction regressions need lower-friction runtime diagnosis.

scenarios.scenario-1: A fully seeded canonical vvoc.json, including the seeded preset map, reports OK.
scenarios.scenario-2: Invalid JSON reports parse error with line/column.
scenarios.scenario-3: Missing required section or invalid field type reports the schema path and error.

V-M-CLI-CONFIG-VALIDATE.wave-follow-up: Run against real config files in the repository.

scenarios.scenario-1: `preset list` shows the default `openai`, `zai`, and `minimax` presets.
scenarios.scenario-2: `preset show openai` renders the stored preset object from canonical vvoc.json.
scenarios.scenario-3: Applying a partial preset only changes the targets listed in that preset and leaves unlisted targets untouched, including top-level OpenCode defaults.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unknown preset names fail with a clear error that includes the available preset names.
scenarios.scenario-5: Malformed `guardian`, `memory-reviewer`, or `default` preset model strings fail through the same validation rules used by `vvoc agent set`.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: Preset application must reuse existing vvoc/OpenCode write helpers instead of introducing a parallel mutation path.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: Preset application must never reset targets that are not explicitly listed in the selected preset.

V-M-CLI-PRESET.wave-follow-up: Consider dynamic shell completion for custom user-defined preset names if the CLI grows a config-discovery completion layer.

scenarios.scenario-1: Lists all plugins when OpenCode config has a populated plugin array.
scenarios.scenario-2: Shows "no plugins" when plugin array is empty.
scenarios.scenario-3: Correctly shows plugin source path for package-based and file-based plugins.

V-M-CLI-PLUGIN-LIST.wave-follow-up: Run against a temp config with known plugin entries.

scenarios.scenario-1: `vvoc patch-provider stepfun-ai` writes `provider.stepfun.options.baseURL = https://api.stepfun.ai/v1` into the global OpenCode config.
scenarios.scenario-2: `vvoc patch-provider zai` writes the `provider.zai-coding-plan.models.glm-4.5-airx` patch into the global OpenCode config without clobbering sibling model entries.
scenarios.scenario-3: Reapplying the same preset is idempotent and reports a kept result.
scenarios.scenario-4: Unsupported preset names fail with a clear list of supported presets.

required-trace-assertions.assertion-1: The command must fail rather than overwrite non-object `provider` or `provider..options` config shapes.
required-trace-assertions.assertion-2: The command must fail rather than overwrite a non-object `provider.` config shape such as `provider.zai-coding-plan`.

V-M-CLI-PATCH-PROVIDER.wave-follow-up: Add more presets only when a concrete endpoint mismatch needs a one-shot patch.

scenarios.scenario-1: Bash completion script contains the current vvoc top-level commands plus nested completions for `config`, `plugin`, `patch-provider`, and the `agent set|unset ` flow including built-in `general` and `explore`.
scenarios.scenario-2: Zsh completion script registers `vvoc` via `#compdef` and `compdef` markers so the installed file works when sourced or loaded from completion paths.
scenarios.scenario-3: Fish completion script is valid fish syntax and exposes nested completions for `config`, `plugin`, `patch-provider`, and the `agent set|unset ` flow including built-in `general` and `explore`.
scenarios.scenario-4: The zsh install flow appends an idempotent loader block that initializes completion support before sourcing `_vvoc`.

V-M-CLI-COMPLETION.wave-follow-up: Validate generated scripts by sourcing/eval in test environment, including the zsh loader path added by `vvoc completion`.

scenarios.scenario-1: Interactive prompts are shown when TTY is available.
scenarios.scenario-2: Ctrl+C aborts cleanly without partial file writes.
scenarios.scenario-3: Re-running init does not require legacy per-feature config files and does not clobber existing valid canonical config.
scenarios.scenario-4: Generated canonical config is valid JSON with the correct versioned schema URL.
scenarios.scenario-5: Non-interactive init seeds the managed `enhancer` primary agent alongside the managed subagent registrations.

V-M-CLI-INIT.wave-follow-up: Test with a temp directory and verify file contents.

scenarios.scenario-1: Reports "already latest" when npm returns same version.
scenarios.scenario-2: When npm returns a newer version, the command runs exactly `bun add -g @osovv/vv-opencode@<latest>` and then runs `vvoc sync` without project scope arguments.
scenarios.scenario-3: Registry or network failure is caught and reported gracefully.
scenarios.scenario-4: Install failure exits non-zero and does not attempt sync.
scenarios.scenario-5: If post-install sync fails or cannot launch, the command still exits successfully with manual `vvoc sync` guidance.
scenarios.scenario-6: Best-effort changelog output preserves placeholder-like strings such as `install-success-plus-sync-warning`.

V-M-CLI-UPGRADE.wave-follow-up: Mock npm registry responses and subprocess execution so upgrade coverage never performs real global installs.

Gate-Phase-1.goal: CLI foundation is buildable and the helper layer has deterministic checks.

required-evidence.evidence-1: Config merge and Guardian config rendering are covered by module-local tests.

Gate-Phase-2.goal: Guardian is packaged from the repo and exported without behavior drift.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `src/plugins/guardian/index.ts` is present and package root exports `GuardianPlugin`.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The packaged `templates/agents/guardian.md` asset is present so Guardian prompt files can be scaffolded into `vvoc/agents/`.

Gate-Phase-3.goal: Explicit memory is packaged from the repo and stored under the vvoc XDG data root with a report-only reviewer agent.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `src/plugins/memory/index.ts` and `src/plugins/memory-store.ts` are present and package root exports `MemoryPlugin`.
required-evidence.evidence-2: The packaged `templates/agents/memory-reviewer.md` asset is present so the memory-reviewer prompt can be scaffolded into `vvoc/agents/`.

Gate-Phase-4.goal: All five CLI enhancement commands are implemented, buildable, and integrated into the CLI.

required-evidence.evidence-1: `vvoc plugin list`, `vvoc config validate`, `vvoc completion`, `vvoc init`, and `vvoc upgrade` all register as CLI commands.
required-evidence.evidence-2: Each command has at least one passing module-level test.
